$schema_note: Documentation-only evidence artifact for task F0-011. This inventory does not authorize any storage-provider selection, migration, backup implementation, or restore execution. It classifies the CURRENT (pre-migration) shape of file-bearing and backup-bearing flows in the repository, bounded to targeted inspection, and proposes (does not decide) migration priority/risk for a future, separately-authorized migration and backup program.
task: F0-011 — Object Storage and Backup Migration Design
phase: F0 — Baseline, Program Control, and Architecture Validation

generated_from.baseline_pr: PR #179 merge commit (docs(architecture): design queue and outbox PoC (F0-010))
generated_from.method: Targeted CodeGraph-equivalent (Glob/Grep/Read, no .codegraph/ index present in this worktree) investigation of server/src/services/fileStorage.ts, server/src/routes/attachments.ts, server/src/routes/imaging.ts, server/src/routes/imagingBridgePublic.ts, server/src/services/privacy/clinicBulkExportPackage.ts, server/src/services/privacy/patientPrivacyExportPackage.ts, server/src/services/privacy/patientAnonymization.ts, server/src/services/privacy/orphanFileInspection.ts, server/src/services/backupService.ts, server/src/jobs/clinicBulkExportCleanupJob.ts, server/src/jobs/patientPrivacyExportCleanupJob.ts, and server/prisma/schema.prisma (PatientAttachment, LabOrderAttachment, ImagingImage, ImagingStudy, ImagingBridgeAgent/Pairing/Binding models).
generated_from.coverage_statement: BOUNDED, not repository-wide. This inventory covers every storage-abstraction call site and every backup mechanism found via targeted inspection of the source roots named in the F0-011 task's CodeGraph-requirement list. It does NOT claim to have found every file-bearing code path in the repository. Four areas are explicitly UNRESOLVED and excluded from this inventory pending a broader scan: (1) WhatsApp/Instagram/SMS inbound/outbound message-media handling — targeted grep found no fileStorage.ts import in server/src/services/whatsapp/, instagram/, or sms/ directories, but those directories were not read in full; (2) clinic logo/branding upload — no such feature was found by targeted grep; (3) email-attachment sending — server/src/services/emailService.ts and emailTemplates.ts were not read in depth; (4) the imagingBridgePublic.ts studies-upload handler (400+ lines) was not read in full, hence flow F011-007 is classified with low confidence. Any storage-shaped flow not listed here should be treated as UNCLASSIFIED, not as 'confirmed absent' or 'confirmed synchronous/safe'.

storage_mode_legend.local_or_s3_dual: Flow is routed through server/src/services/fileStorage.ts, which is dual-mode: local disk when S3_BUCKET is unset (current production state), S3-compatible object storage when set (not currently configured in production)
storage_mode_legend.local_only_unabstracted: Flow writes/reads a fixed local filesystem path with no storage-abstraction layer and no S3-mode equivalent (PostgreSQL backup only)
storage_mode_legend.not_found: No implementation exists in the repository for this storage class; listed to record a confirmed gap, not an observed flow
storage_mode_legend.ambiguous: Insufficient evidence in this bounded investigation to classify storage mode with confidence

migration_priority_legend.HIGH: Patient-facing clinical data, currently uncovered by any backup, or both
migration_priority_legend.MEDIUM: Tenant-scoped operational data with partial mitigations already in place (e.g. short TTL, cleanup job)
migration_priority_legend.LOW: Low sensitivity, low volume, or already time-bound by an existing retention mechanism
migration_priority_legend.N/A: Not a migration candidate (e.g. a confirmed gap with no existing implementation to migrate, or a flow this document explicitly does not propose migrating)

flows.name: Patient attachment upload
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: Resolved via validateAndGetClinicIdScope before any storage call; PatientAttachment has no direct organizationId/clinicId column — scope is inherited from the parent Patient row at write time
flows.upload_path: POST /patients/:patientId/attachments (attachments.ts:101-180) — multer memoryStorage, magic-byte validation (isAllowedFileSignature, :137), saveFile(), DB row created, stored file rolled back if DB insert fails (:174-177)
flows.authorization_path: validateAndGetClinicIdScope + scoped Prisma findFirst on every route; never trusts req.user.clinicId directly
flows.delete_path: DELETE /patients/:patientId/attachments/:id (attachments.ts:382-461) — atomic deleteMany({legalHold:false}) closes a TOCTOU window, then deleteFile(); documented as the ONLY attachment-delete path in the codebase (comment, attachments.ts:361)
flows.retention_behavior: Persistent, no automatic expiry; legalHold field blocks deletion when true
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED — file bytes are not included in any evidenced backup process; only the DB row (via PostgreSQL backup) is covered
flows.integrity_mechanism: None persisted (no checksum column on PatientAttachment); magic-byte validation is upload-time only, not an ongoing integrity check
flows.encryption_evidence: None evidenced at rest (local disk, no encryption layer found); TLS in transit assumed via existing HTTPS termination, not independently re-verified in this task
flows.audit_evidence: No AuditLog entry evidenced for upload/download in this bounded pass (legal-hold PATCH does write an audit log, attachments.ts:288-356)
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM — patient-facing clinical data; key format already PHI-safe, reducing migration-specific PHI-exposure risk
flows.rollback_requirement: Any migration must preserve local-fallback read capability until backfill is checksum-verified (see design doc §10)
unresolved_questions: Whether upload/download AuditLog coverage should be added independent of any storage migration

flows.name: Patient attachment preview (inline)
flows.file_type: Subset of F011-001's types gated by isInlinePreviewable
flows.current_path_key_format: Same as F011-001 (reads the same stored key)
flows.authorization_path: Same scoped findFirst pattern as F011-001
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED (shares the same underlying object as F011-001)
flows.audit_evidence: Not evidenced in this bounded pass
flows.migration_risk: LOW — read-only, no independent migration surface beyond F011-001's

flows.name: Patient attachment legal hold and delete
flows.authorization_path: PATCH legal-hold: OWNER/ORG_ADMIN only, requires a reason, writes writeAuditLog (attachments.ts:288-356); DELETE: same scoped-findFirst pattern as F011-001
flows.delete_path: DELETE /patients/:patientId/attachments/:id — see F011-001 for the atomic guard detail
flows.retention_behavior: legalHold=true blocks the only delete path; storageVerifiedMissingAt is set by orphanFileInspection.ts when a DB row's file is confirmed missing (dry-run only, never auto-remediated)
flows.backup_coverage: N/A (this is a control/delete flow, not a storage-bearing one)
flows.integrity_mechanism: orphanFileInspection.ts (server/src/services/privacy/orphanFileInspection.ts, 136 lines) — bounded, single-patient, dry-run only; classifies dbRowPhysicalMissing vs activeLinkedObject via fileExists(); reverse-orphan detection (object exists, no DB row) NOT implemented, would require listObjects
flows.migration_risk: N/A — this is a control-flow row documenting the deletion/integrity mechanism referenced by F011-001, not a separate migration candidate
unresolved_questions: Reverse-orphan detection (object exists, no DB row) is a named future requirement, not yet built (see design doc §7.2)

flows.name: Lab order attachment lifecycle (upload/download/delete)
flows.domain_owner: Lab Orders / Attachments (structurally identical route pattern to attachments.ts, per LabOrderAttachment model)
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: Same scoped-resolution pattern as PatientAttachment (per schema field parity)
flows.file_type: Same allowlist as PatientAttachment (structurally identical model)
flows.approx_size_class: small, <=10MB (assumed parity with PatientAttachment; not independently re-verified per-route in this pass)
flows.current_path_key_format: Same buildStorageKey convention as F011-001 (assumed parity; route file not independently re-read in this pass beyond the schema model)
flows.upload_path: Not independently re-traced route-by-route in this bounded pass; inferred from schema field parity with PatientAttachment
flows.retention_behavior: Same field shape as PatientAttachment (legalHold present)
flows.audit_evidence: Not independently re-verified in this pass
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM — same class of risk as F011-001, lower confidence since the route file itself was not independently re-read
unresolved_questions: Route-level behavior was inferred from schema parity with PatientAttachment, not independently re-traced line-by-line — a future pass should confirm this directly

flows.name: Imaging image upload/link
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: Resolved via role + clinic scope checks in imaging.ts routes
flows.approx_size_class: medium-large (not independently measured in this pass; imaging files are plausibly larger than the 10MB attachment cap per a separate MAX_FILE_MB limit)
flows.current_path_key_format: ImagingImage.filePath — schema comment: "depolama anahtarı — asla public URL üretilmez" (a storage key, never a public URL)
flows.upload_path: imaging.ts upload routes — multer memoryStorage (imaging.ts:67-75), "no temp file ever written to disk" per code comment
flows.download_path: GET imaging download/preview (imaging.ts:748-800) — openFileStream(image.filePath), Cache-Control: private, no-store, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
flows.authorization_path: Role + clinic scope gate, plus auditImaging() write on every view
flows.delete_path: Not found — ImagingStudy is documented as immutable ("tanısal veri hiç hard-delete edilmez", schema comment, schema.prisma:2450-2497); status moves to archived, not hard-deleted
flows.retention_behavior: Conservative-retain; no imaging-specific lifecycle category enum exists yet (per patientAnonymization.ts's own documented pending-work note)
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED — same file-tree-backup gap as F011-001, at higher sensitivity and plausibly larger scale
flows.integrity_mechanism: None evidenced beyond MIME allowlist at upload time
flows.audit_evidence: auditImaging() on every view (imaging.ts:770) — stronger audit coverage than the attachment path
flows.migration_risk: HIGH — highest sensitivity classification in this inventory, plausibly largest file sizes, and the file-tree backup gap that applies to F011-001 applies here at greater severity
flows.rollback_requirement: Design doc §11 — no lossy compression of originals, checksum-verified backfill mandatory before any local-fallback removal
unresolved_questions: Exact file-size distribution not measured in this pass
DICOM-tag-level de-identification requirement not resolved (design doc §11)

flows.name: Imaging image download/preview (patient-facing and clinician-facing views)
flows.download_path: Same route as documented in F011-005's download_path
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED (shares the same underlying object as F011-005)
flows.migration_risk: LOW — read-only, no independent migration surface beyond F011-005's

flows.name: Imaging bridge study ingest (device-pushed upload)
flows.sensitivity_classification: VERY_HIGH if confirmed synchronous — imaging PHI (DICOM/CBCT study data) potentially transferred inline in an HTTP request
flows.file_type: DICOM/CBCT (narrow Part-10 signature detection only, per imaging.ts module doc comment)
flows.approx_size_class: AMBIGUOUS — plausibly large (multi-file DICOM studies), not measured
flows.current_path_key_format: Likely follows the same fileStorage.ts convention as F011-005, not independently confirmed in this pass
flows.upload_path: POST /imaging/bridge/studies (imagingBridgePublic.ts:199) — synchronous push from the on-prem bridge agent; ingestKey de-dup keyed by clinicId+sha256 (schema.prisma:2475-2480) confirmed, but the full 400+ line handler was not read in full
flows.download_path: AMBIGUOUS — not confirmed in this pass
flows.authorization_path: Agent-pairing-based, not independently re-verified beyond the pairing endpoint
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED (same file-tree-backup gap as all other flows, if this flow does write to fileStorage.ts as assumed)
flows.integrity_mechanism: sha256-based ingestKey dedup confirmed (schema.prisma:2475-2480) — the strongest existing checksum precedent found anywhere in this inventory
flows.audit_evidence: An 'imaging_bridge_heartbeat_first_seen' audit-style log was found for the heartbeat path (imagingBridgePublic.ts:184); not confirmed for the studies-upload path specifically
flows.migration_risk: AMBIGUOUS — flagged explicitly for a future targeted investigation before any migration risk rating can be assigned
unresolved_questions: Full imagingBridgePublic.ts studies-upload handler not read in full
Whether this flow uses fileStorage.ts at all, vs. a separate path, not confirmed

flows.name: Clinic bulk export archive generation (KVKK-HIGH-004)
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: scope.clinicId / scope.organizationId, re-validated at claim time and generation time
flows.sensitivity_classification: VERY_HIGH — full clinic-wide structured + attachment export
flows.approx_size_class: large (whole-clinic export, size-limited by SIZE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED failure code)
flows.upload_path: Staged in EXPORT_TEMP_DIR (os.tmpdir()/diskliniks-export-tmp, mode 0700, symlink/ownership-verified), then promoted atomically via saveFileFromPath() (same-fs rename or EXDEV-safe copy-then-rename via a .partial-<uuid> sibling)
flows.authorization_path: Bearer-token model: crypto.randomBytes(32) raw token, only SHA-256 hash persisted (downloadTokenHash), atomic single claim, expiresAt
flows.delete_path: clinicBulkExportCleanupJob.ts (cron */15 * * * *, withJobLock) expires ready rows past expiresAt and deletes storage objects
flows.retention_behavior: Short TTL, enforced by the cleanup job every 15 minutes
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED (and arguably should not be — this is a short-TTL derived artifact, not a source-of-truth record; see design doc §9.3)
flows.integrity_mechanism: ZIP_INTEGRITY_FAILED is a named failure code, implying an integrity check exists at generation time; not independently confirmed as a persisted checksum
flows.audit_evidence: AuditLog at creation-disabled attempt, step-up failures, and three distinct download outcomes, some via writeAuditLogInTx (transactional)
flows.migration_risk: LOW — short-lived by design, already the strongest existing precedent for atomic promote/cleanup in the codebase
flows.rollback_requirement: Minimal — short TTL means an interrupted migration self-resolves via the existing cleanup job

flows.name: Clinic bulk export download (token-gated)
flows.download_path: Download endpoint validated against the one-time bearer token; confirms the file opens before claiming the token (avoids burning a token on a storage failure)

flows.name: Patient privacy export archive generation and download
flows.sensitivity_classification: VERY_HIGH — full patient record including appointments, payments, treatment cases
flows.file_type: ZIP (and a JSON export path with no storage-key equivalent, per design doc §5's inventory notes)
flows.current_path_key_format: Same buildExportStorageKey convention as F011-008, patient-export-specific ID
flows.upload_path: Same EXPORT_TEMP_DIR staging + saveFileFromPath() promotion pattern as F011-008
flows.download_path: One-time download token (claimExportDownload), same bearer-token model as F011-009
flows.authorization_path: Same bearer-token model; JSON export path (non-ZIP) has no token, naturally repeatable
flows.delete_path: patientPrivacyExportCleanupJob.ts (15-minute sweep, 1-hour TTL, env-gated by PATIENT_PRIVACY_EXPORT_CLEANUP_ENABLED)
flows.retention_behavior: 1-hour TTL, shorter than the clinic bulk export's
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED (same reasoning as F011-008 — short-TTL derived artifact)
flows.integrity_mechanism: ExportGenerationInProgressError/ExportLeaseLostError imply a generation-lease mechanism at the service layer
flows.audit_evidence: AuditLog for create and three-state ZIP download outcome, same pattern as F011-008
unresolved_questions: patientPrivacyExportCleanupJob.ts is env-gated (PATIENT_PRIVACY_EXPORT_CLEANUP_ENABLED) — production value of this flag not verified in this task

flows.name: Export temp staging directory (pre-promotion buffer)
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: Implicit via the filename pattern only (EXPORT_TEMP_FILE_PATTERN); not itself tenant-scoped at the filesystem level
flows.sensitivity_classification: VERY_HIGH (holds the same content as F011-008/F011-010 during generation)
flows.current_storage_mode: local_only_by_design_staging (always local, regardless of S3 mode — this is a pre-promotion buffer, not final storage)
flows.current_path_key_format: os.tmpdir()/diskliniks-export-tmp/<pattern>, directory created with mode 0700, fail-closed symlink/ownership verification (ensureExportTempDir)
flows.upload_path: Internal only — written by clinicBulkExportPackage.ts / patientPrivacyExportPackage.ts during generation
flows.download_path: N/A — never directly served; only the promoted final object (F011-008/F011-010) is downloadable
flows.delete_path: cleanupStaleLocalExportPartialFiles (fileStorage.ts) sweeps stale .partial-* files older than 30 minutes, DB-row-gated fail-closed
flows.backup_coverage: N/A — deliberately not a backup target (transient by design)
flows.integrity_mechanism: Atomic rename / EXDEV-safe copy-then-rename on promotion avoids partial-file exposure at the final key
flows.encryption_evidence: Not evidenced; directory permissions (0700) are the primary control
flows.audit_evidence: Not evidenced for the staging step itself
flows.migration_risk: N/A — this is process-internal plumbing, not a migration candidate itself

flows.name: Export cleanup jobs (clinic bulk + patient privacy, combined retention enforcement)
flows.domain_owner: Privacy / Bulk Export, Privacy / Patient Export (server/src/jobs/clinicBulkExportCleanupJob.ts, server/src/jobs/patientPrivacyExportCleanupJob.ts)
flows.organization_clinic_linkage: Global sweep across all clinics/patients' archives (not per-tenant scheduled runs)
flows.sensitivity_classification: MEDIUM — operates on already-exported archive metadata/files, not live patient records directly
flows.delete_path: See F011-008/F011-010's delete_path fields — this row documents the job mechanism itself
flows.backup_coverage: N/A (deletion mechanism, not a storage-bearing flow)
flows.audit_evidence: Not evidenced for the sweep operation itself
flows.migration_risk: N/A — must be preserved as-is by any future migration (§10 of the design doc); not itself a migration target

flows.name: PostgreSQL database backup
flows.sensitivity_classification: VERY_HIGH — the entire application database, including all PHI
flows.file_type: pg_dump-style archive (exact format not confirmed — the underlying script is not in the repository)
flows.current_path_key_format: BACKUP_DIR=/root/noramedi-backups (backupService.ts constant); BACKUP_SCRIPT=/usr/local/sbin/noramedi-db-backup.sh; BACKUP_LOG=/var/log/noramedi-db-backup.log; cron unit /etc/cron.d/noramedi-db-backup — none of these exist inside the repository, only the Node wrapper that shells out to them
flows.upload_path: N/A — externally triggered by cron, not an application upload path
flows.authorization_path: runRestoreTest() is admin-triggered, authenticated via platformAdmin.ts pattern
flows.delete_path: RETENTION_DAYS=7 declared in backupService.ts; enforcement mechanism is external to the repository and unverified
flows.backup_coverage: This IS the backup mechanism for the database; backup directory is on the SAME HOST as the database (R-030), no offsite copy found
flows.integrity_mechanism: runRestoreTest() (backupService.ts:167-277): createdb -> pg_restore --no-privileges --no-owner -> row-count sanity queries -> dropdb — a genuine periodic-restore-verification capability, but no durable evidence it has ever actually been run (R-032)
flows.encryption_evidence: Not evidenced — the backup script itself is not in the repository, so its encryption behavior (if any) could not be inspected
flows.audit_evidence: Not evidenced for the backup process itself
flows.migration_risk: HIGH — closing R-030 (offsite copy) and R-031 (PITR) both touch this flow directly
flows.rollback_requirement: N/A — this document does not propose changing the backup mechanism, only flags the gaps
unresolved_questions: Exact backup script content, format, and any encryption is unknown — the script itself is not in the repository
Whether RETENTION_DAYS=7 is actually enforced was not independently verified

flows.name: File-tree / object-storage backup
flows.sensitivity_classification: N/A — this row documents a confirmed absence, not an observed flow
flows.backup_coverage: NOT_COVERED — this row exists specifically to record that no file-tree backup script, job, or process was found in the inspected storage, backup, attachment, imaging, export, and job paths (backupService.ts, fileStorage.ts, attachments.ts, imaging.ts, imagingBridgePublic.ts, services/privacy/*, export-related jobs) — a bounded finding, not a repository-wide search — and PRODUCTION_TOPOLOGY.md's own evidence indicates uploads/ inclusion in the DB backup pipeline is unconfirmed and likely absent
flows.migration_risk: N/A — this is the single highest-priority gap named in the design document's backup architecture section (§9), but is recorded here as a confirmed absence, not a migration candidate
unresolved_questions: Direct production evidence (not repository inspection) would be needed to fully rule out an undocumented ad hoc file-tree backup process

counts.note: by_storage_mode sums to 14, matching total_flows_classified. F011-011 (export temp staging) is recorded under its own descriptive category (local_only_by_design_staging) since it is always-local pre-promotion buffer, distinct from the dual-mode final-storage flows.
